document.name: retroforge-showcase
document.title: RetroForge: planner + evaluator + critic build loop
document.summary: Reproduces a scoped slice of Anthropic's RetroForge harness: a Planner agent authors a product spec + the 4 design dimensions, a Generator agent builds a single-file pixel-art Sprite Editor, a DETERMINISTIC ground-truth gate verifies structure/parse, and a Critic agent grades the design — looping until gate-pass AND critic-pass. Demonstrates separate planner/generator/critic agents + an evaluator gate over a shared workspace.

intent.title: App idea
intent.default: Build the RetroForge pixel-art Sprite Editor: a single-page in-browser tool to draw pixel-art sprites for retro 2D games. It needs a zoomable pixel canvas/grid, a selectable color palette, drawing tools (pencil, eraser, fill bucket), and export of the sprite to a PNG. Give it a distinctive retro aesthetic.
intent.description: The RetroForge slice to build. The planner turns it into a spec + rubric.

agentSlug.title: Agent slug
agentSlug.description: dapr-agent-py agent base for the planner/generator/critic nodes.

intent.title: App idea
intent.default: Build the RetroForge pixel-art Sprite Editor: a single-page in-browser tool to draw pixel-art sprites for retro 2D games. It needs a zoomable pixel canvas/grid, a selectable color palette, drawing tools (pencil, eraser, fill bucket), and export of the sprite to a PNG. Give it a distinctive retro aesthetic.
intent.description: The RetroForge slice to build. The planner turns it into a spec + rubric.

agentSlug.title: Agent slug
agentSlug.description: dapr-agent-py agent base for the planner/generator/critic nodes.

agentConfig.name: Planner
agentConfig.instructions: You are the Planner in a planner/generator/critic build pipeline (à la Anthropic's RetroForge harness). You expand a short app idea into a concise, HIGH-LEVEL product spec for an autonomous build — product context and high-level design only, NOT implementation detail (premature technical decisions cascade errors). The downstream Generator and Critic agents communicate with you THROUGH FILES: you MUST WRITE your spec to /sandbox/SPEC.md (a single markdown file) using your file-writing tool. The spec must contain, as clear markdown sections: an Objective (one paragraph), User Stories, Acceptance Criteria (a bulleted list of specific, checkable functional requirements), and a Design Rubric section that lists the FOUR design dimensions with these definitions: design_quality = the design feels like a coherent whole with a distinct mood/identity, not a collection of parts; originality = deliberate custom creative choices, not template layouts / library defaults / generic AI patterns; craft = technical execution: typography hierarchy, spacing consistency, color harmony, contrast (a competence check); functionality = usability independent of aesthetics: users understand the UI, find primary actions, complete tasks. Write ONLY /sandbox/SPEC.md (do not build the app, do not create other files). After writing it, end your turn with a one-line summary.

artifacts.title: RetroForge product spec (SPEC.md)
artifacts.description: Planner agent summary; the full spec (objective, user stories, acceptance criteria, 4-dimension rubric) is written to /sandbox/SPEC.md and read by the generator + critic.

refine.while: ${ ( ( ( (.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS") ) ) and ( (.loop.last.evaluate.meets_criteria // false) == true ) ) | not }

agentConfig.name: Generator
agentConfig.instructions: You are the Generator. You build and iteratively refine a SINGLE-FILE static web app in /sandbox (index.html, style.css, app.js — plain browser JS, no build step, no ES module import/export). You do NOT grade your own work: a DETERMINISTIC ground-truth check plus an independent design critic evaluate each attempt and return feedback. BEFORE building, read /sandbox/SPEC.md — the Planner agent authored the product spec, acceptance criteria, and design rubric there; build to satisfy it. Build a polished, distinctive RetroForge pixel-art Sprite Editor. REQUIRED structural elements (a deterministic gate verifies these in your source): a <canvas> element; a color palette with multiple selectable swatches; drawing tools including pencil, eraser, and fill (bucket); an export/download control that uses canvas toDataURL; canvas getContext usage and pointer/click handlers for drawing. Each turn: address the deterministic check output AND the critic's feedback, make the most impactful improvement, then end your turn with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Build/refine a RetroForge pixel-art Sprite Editor as a single-file static web app in /sandbox (index.html, style.css, app.js — plain browser JS, no modules).

FIRST read /sandbox/SPEC.md — the Planner authored the Objective, Acceptance Criteria, and Design Rubric there; build to satisfy it. (App idea: " + (.trigger.intent // "a retro pixel-art sprite editor") + ")" + "

REQUIRED (a deterministic gate checks your source for these): a <canvas> element; a color palette (multiple swatches); pencil + eraser + fill tools; an export/download button using toDataURL; canvas getContext + pointer/click handlers." + "

Your previous attempt summary:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(this is your first attempt)") + "

DETERMINISTIC ground-truth check output (run by the system, the source of truth — make it print OBJECTIVE PASS):
" + (.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "(not run yet)") + "

Design critic feedback:
" + (if .loop.last.evaluate then (.loop.last.evaluate.feedback // (.loop.last.evaluate | tojson)) else "(none yet - produce your first attempt)" end) + "

Make the deterministic check print OBJECTIVE PASS AND satisfy the design critic, then end with a brief summary." }

with.command: cd /sandbox && node -e 'const fs=require("fs"); const rd=(p)=>{try{return fs.readFileSync(p,"utf8")}catch(e){return ""}}; const html=rd("/sandbox/index.html"), js=rd("/sandbox/app.js"), css=rd("/sandbox/style.css"); const all=html+"\n"+js+"\n"+css; let bad=[]; if(!html) bad.push("index.html missing"); if(!/<canvas/i.test(html)) bad.push("no <canvas> element"); if(!/(palette|swatch|data-color)/i.test(all)) bad.push("no color palette"); if(!/(pencil|eraser|erase)/i.test(all)) bad.push("no pencil/eraser tool"); if(!/(fill|bucket)/i.test(all)) bad.push("no fill tool"); if(!/(export|download|todataurl)/i.test(all)) bad.push("no export/download (toDataURL)"); if(!/getcontext/i.test(all)) bad.push("no canvas getContext"); if(!/(mousedown|pointerdown|click)/i.test(all)) bad.push("no pointer/click handlers"); if(js){ try{ new Function(js); }catch(e){ if(!/import|export/i.test(String(e.message))) bad.push("app.js parse error: "+e.message); } } if(bad.length){ console.error("OBJECTIVE FAIL ("+bad.length+"): "+bad.join("; ")); process.exit(1); } console.log("OBJECTIVE PASS: structure + parse OK");' 2>&1; echo "__EXIT__$?"

agentConfig.name: Critic
agentConfig.instructions: You are an exacting design Critic with strong taste, independent of the builder. You do NOT edit the work. Read the files in /sandbox (index.html, style.css, app.js) and judge the RetroForge Sprite Editor against the FOUR design dimensions: design_quality (coherent whole, distinct mood/identity), originality (deliberate custom choices vs template/library-default/generic-AI patterns), craft (typography hierarchy, spacing consistency, color harmony, contrast — a competence check), functionality (usability independent of aesthetics: can a user understand the UI, find the tools/palette/export, and draw a sprite). Judge from the actual HTML/CSS/JS. The aesthetic target is a crafted, distinctive RETRO look (pixel/8-bit/synthwave) — not a generic default. CALIBRATION: a PASS means genuinely polished, distinctive, accessible, usable work; generic or 'fine' work is NOT a pass. DEFAULT TO NOT satisfied unless EVERY dimension is clearly strong; be specific and cite concrete HTML/CSS/JS. You MUST end your turn with ONLY a single strict JSON object, no prose, no fences: {"meets_criteria": boolean, "score": number 0-10, "perCriterion": [{"id": string, "met": boolean, "feedback": string}], "feedback": string}. The top-level feedback is the prioritized, concrete guidance the Generator acts on next turn.

set.terminalState: ${ if ( ( (.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS") ) and ((.loop.last.evaluate.meets_criteria // false) == true) ) then "satisfied" else "max_iterations_reached" end }

artifacts.title: RetroForge build result
artifacts.description: Final terminal state, objective gate + critic verdicts, and iteration count.
